question.text: Mrs. Rao receives total dividend (IDCW) of Rs. 8,000 from a mutual fund house during the year. How much TDS will the fund house deduct?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** TDS at 10% is deducted on mutual fund dividend (IDCW) when the total dividend from a single fund house exceeds Rs. 5,000 in a financial year. Since Mrs. Rao received Rs. 8,000 (which exceeds Rs. 5,000), TDS = 10% of Rs. 8,000 = Rs. 800. **The Trap:** TDS is applied on the full dividend amount of Rs. 8,000, not just on the excess above Rs. 5,000. The Rs. 5,000 is only the threshold to determine whether TDS applies or not. If the total had been Rs. 4,500, no TDS would be deducted. **Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in V-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Retired investors with income below taxable limits should submit Form 15H to the fund house to avoid TDS deduction on dividends. This ensures they receive the full dividend amount without any deduction.
